name: Embalse de Villalba de los Barros y Ermita de San Juan – circular desde La Parra
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Parra offers access to the diverse natural landscapes of Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by extensive dehesas, open oak forests, and rolling hills, providing varied terrain for cycling. Trails often traverse a mix of woodlands and pastures, with some routes featuring views of local reservoirs. This topography supports a range of mountain bike trails, from undulating paths to more challenging ascents. Best mountain bike trails around La Parra The most popular mountain bike trail is Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales — circular por el Embalse de Nogales, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route connects historic towns and offers views of the Nogales Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra del Pico, Rivera del Guadajira y Sierra Caliza – circular desde Feria, a moderate 16.5 miles (26.5 km) path. This route explores varied terrain including the Sierra del Pico and Sierra Caliza. Local mountain bikers also love the Embalse de Villalba de los Barros y Ermita de San Juan – circular desde La Parra, a 32.7 miles (52.7 km) trail leading through the area around the Villalba de los Barros Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around La Parra is defined by dehesas, oak forests, and rolling hills. The network of routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in La Parra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 7 reviews. More than 120 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Parra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around La Parra?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around La Parra, offering a wide range of experiences. You'll find routes suitable for various skill levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents and descents through the diverse landscapes of Extremadura.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in La Parra?
A: Yes, La Parra offers several easy mountain bike trails, with 4 routes specifically categorized as easy. Additionally, many of the 43 moderate routes feature rolling hills and scenic dehesas, which can be enjoyable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These trails often provide picturesque views without overly technical sections.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced mountain bikers expect around La Parra?
A: For advanced mountain bikers, there are 9 difficult routes around La Parra. These trails often involve more significant elevation changes and technical sections, traversing the region's varied terrain including mountain ranges and deeper valleys, providing a more demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around La Parra are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359947973/salvatierra-a-salvaleon-a-nogales-circular-por-el-embalse-de-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales — circular por el Embalse de Nogales&lt;/a&gt;, which connects historic towns and offers views of the Nogales Reservoir.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike trails near La Parra?
A: If you're looking for longer rides, the area offers several options. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030775861/embalse-de-villalba-de-los-barros-y-ermita-de-san-juan-circular-desde-la-parra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Villalba de los Barros y Ermita de San Juan – circular desde La Parra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.7-mile (52.7 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 50 minutes to complete, leading through the area around the Villalba de los Barros Reservoir.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I see while mountain biking around La Parra?
A: Mountain biking around La Parra will immerse you in the characteristic landscapes of Extremadura. You'll primarily encounter 'dehesas' – open oak forests interspersed with pastures – along with rolling hills, and sometimes views of local reservoirs like the Nogales Reservoir. The region also features chestnut, cork oak, and holm oak forests, offering a diverse and scenic backdrop for your rides.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in La Parra?
A: The mountain bike trails around La Parra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 7 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the dehesas and oak forests, and the opportunity to explore both natural and historical sites along the routes.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, many routes integrate natural beauty with historical sites. For example, the area features several castles. You might encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6052765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villalba de los Barros Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1124589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Hermitage of La Parra&lt;/a&gt; and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olive Tree of La Tapada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Parra?
A: Extremadura generally offers good conditions for outdoor activities for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if visiting during this season. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to ride year-round, though some trails might be affected by rain.

Q: Are there any hidden gem mountain bike trails around La Parra?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1033566814/sierra-del-pico-rivera-del-guadajira-y-sierra-caliza-circular-desde-feria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Pico, Rivera del Guadajira y Sierra Caliza – circular desde Feria&lt;/a&gt; offer a less-trafficked experience. This moderate 16.5-mile path explores varied terrain including the Sierra del Pico and Sierra Caliza, providing a sense of discovery away from the more popular routes.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass by water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region is home to numerous rivers and reservoirs that enhance the cycling experience. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359947973/salvatierra-a-salvaleon-a-nogales-circular-por-el-embalse-de-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales — circular por el Embalse de Nogales&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views of the Nogales Reservoir, providing a refreshing element to your ride.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, many routes in the region start from towns or villages like La Parra, Feria, or Salvatierra de los Barros, where public parking is generally available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.
tips: Typical landscape through Extremadura meadows with a pond where you can see local birds

Built on the bed of the Guadajira River and near the town of Villalba de los Barros, the dam gives us precious views as we meander along the shore. Pastures grow in the surroundings that are used by the sheep farms present in the area. On the horizon of the swamp you can see the castle of Feria.
Located on the outskirts of the town of La Parra and in the middle of the beautiful landscape of the valley, we find the beautiful Hermitage of San Juan, so loved by the residents.

waypoints.name: San Juan Hermitage of La Parra
